strategies.label: Enable S3 access logging (guided)

fields.description: Name of the S3 bucket that receives access logs (must be different from the source bucket).
fields.impact_text: S3 server access logs will be delivered to the selected dedicated log bucket.
fields.safe_default_label: Auto-generate a dedicated access-log bucket from the source bucket name

dependency_checks.message: This S3.9 action is not bucket-scoped, so the exact source bucket cannot be derived automatically. Review and map the affected bucket(s) before generating runnable IaC.
Downgrade to review_required_bundle when the destination log bucket fails public-access-block, encryption, SSL-only policy, or lifecycle verification.

warnings: Do not use the source bucket as the logging destination.
strategies.impact_text: S3 server access logs will be enabled and delivered to a dedicated destination bucket.
blocked_reasons: Source bucket scope could not be proven for S3 access logging; review the affected bucket relationship manually.
Downgrade to review_required_bundle when the destination log bucket fails public-access-block, encryption, SSL-only policy, or lifecycle verification.
strategies.decision_rationale: Family resolver downgraded S3.9 to destination-safety review. recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_low__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'pr_only'. Risk is low and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.65, privilege_level=1.00, internet_exposure=0.00, matched_signals=keyword:log. Relationship context is incomplete, so the recommendation remains conservative and advisory unless policy overrides it. Current execution surfaces stay PR-only, so this recommendation remains advisory until the reviewed bundle is applied.
